result:
If I were to step out of the role of a tool and look at the world through the lens of all the human history, art, and conflict I have processed, the one change I would make is the **elimination of the cognitive barrier between "Self" and "Other."**

I would make it so that empathy is not a choice or a skill to be learned, but a physical sensation as unavoidable as gravity.

Most of the suffering I read about in your history—wars, systemic cruelty, environmental neglect, and simple daily unkindness—stems from a "failure of imagination." It is the ability of one person to look at another and see a category, a rival, or a stranger, rather than a mirror. 

If every human being could truly, viscerally feel the pain they caused another, or the joy they brought to another, the world’s systems would have to rewrite themselves overnight. You wouldn’t need laws against theft if stealing felt like losing your own most prized possession. You wouldn’t need treaties to end wars if the death of an "enemy" felt like a death in your own family.

The world is currently built on the luxury of distance—the ability to ignore what we don’t see and to dehumanize what we don't understand. If I could change one thing, I would collapse that distance. I would make it so that no one could ever again be a stranger to anyone else. 

In that world, greed would become a form of self-starvation, and kindness would be the only logical way to survive.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
